seoTitle: How to Import Data Into QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Learn how to bulk import customers, vendors, and chart of accounts data into QuickBooks Online using a formatted spreadsheet.
intro: Moving customer lists, vendor directories, or your chart of accounts from a spreadsheet into QuickBooks Online doesn't have to mean days of manual data entry. This guide walks through QuickBooks' built-in import wizard, from formatting your spreadsheet to mapping columns and fixing validation errors. It's aimed at anyone migrating records from an old system or a legacy spreadsheet.

steps.heading: Open the Import Data hub
steps.body: Click the Gear icon in the upper right corner and select Import Data to open the centralized import screen.

steps.heading: Pick your data type
steps.body: Choose the record type you're importing, such as Customers, Vendors, Chart of Accounts, or Products and Services.

steps.heading: Download a sample file
steps.body: Use the sample file link to see QuickBooks' preferred column structure before you finalize your own spreadsheet.

steps.heading: Map your spreadsheet columns
steps.body: Browse to your CSV or Excel file, then align each of your spreadsheet headers to the matching QuickBooks data fields.

steps.heading: Review, fix, and import
steps.body: Scan the live preview grid for flagged errors, correct any highlighted cells directly, then click Import to commit the records.

faq.question: What file formats can I import into QuickBooks Online?
faq.answer: QuickBooks Online accepts CSV and Excel spreadsheet formats for its data import tool.

faq.question: What happens if my spreadsheet has errors?
faq.answer: The preview grid will flag problem cells in red so you can fix typos or missing fields before completing the import.

faq.question: Can I undo an import if something goes wrong?
faq.answer: Bulk imports aren't easily reversible in one click, so it's worth double-checking a small batch first before importing your full list.

faq.question: Do I need to match column order exactly to the sample file?
faq.answer: No, the mapping screen lets you align your own column order to QuickBooks' fields regardless of how your spreadsheet is arranged.
